landmark_cases.holding: Warrant required to search cell phone incident to arrest
landmark_cases.implication: No cell phone search without warrant, regardless of arrest

landmark_cases.holding: Warrant required for historical CSLI (cell-site location information)
landmark_cases.implication: Third-party doctrine does not apply to CSLI spanning 7+ days

landmark_cases.holding: Attaching GPS tracker to vehicle is a Fourth Amendment search
landmark_cases.implication: Physical installation of tracking device requires warrant

landmark_cases.holding: Thermal imaging of home requires warrant
Warrant required for email content (6th Circuit)
landmark_cases.implication: Email has reasonable expectation of privacy; SCA compelled disclosure = 4th Am. violation

landmark_cases.holding: Fourth Amendment protects people, not places; reasonable expectation of privacy test
landmark_cases.implication: Foundational test for all electronic surveillance

landmark_cases.holding: No REP in numbers dialed (pen register); third-party doctrine
landmark_cases.implication: Basis for SCA pen register provisions — but see Carpenter

landmark_cases.holding: 11th Circuit: historical CSLI obtainable with D-order (pre-Carpenter)
landmark_cases.implication: Superseded by Carpenter for federal investigations
